Local Favorites — Silver Lake
Los Angeles, CA

Not just a bookstore.
A place where people meet, connect, and keep coming back.

A Los Feliz staple since 1996, @skylightbooks sits just below Griffith Park with a curated mix of fiction, art, film, and culture. Easy to pop into after dinner, harder to leave without a stack.

Don’t miss their free author events, happening most nights of the week.

Local Favorites — Los Feliz

📍Skylight Books
1818 N Vermont Ave, Los Angeles

You can walk down Sunset and hit 10 different coffee shops.
Here’s the one that feels like home.

@handlescoffee is a Silver Lake go-to with a loyal following and a menu people know by heart.

Order:
• Handles Coffee (iced)
• Handles Matcha
• Silver Lake Latte (orange mocha twist)

Local Favorites — Silver Lake

📍Handles Coffee

✈️ WHERE TO GO IN MAY ✈️ Your next trip, based on what you're craving: 

Water: Barbados or Belize
Cities: Thessaloniki, Greece
Outdoors: Newfoundland, Canada or North Sikkim, India
Slow travel: Uzbekistan or Norway
Food: Murcia, Spain or Bali, Indonesia
Wildlife: Chobe and the Okavango, Botswana

GUIDE TO SPAIN 🇪🇸 In Cádiz, “watch a flamenco performance with local aficionados at the cavernous Peña Flamenca La Perla,” says Lonely Planet writer @anna.cohen.kaminski. Or, opt for a more touristy tablao (choreographed flamenco show) at the intimate La Cava. Express your appreciation by shouting “Viva la máquina escribir!” or “Long live the typewriter!” – a compliment dating back to an age when audiences thought that the noise made by the dancers’ rapid footwork resembled the clack of a typewriter. Tap the link in bio for all things #BestInTravel, our top places to visit this year 🔗

"This is Buenos Aires as I experience it: elegant, beautiful, intense and chaotic all at once – like a first love that stays with you forever," says Lonely Planet Correspondent @gabitofranco of @tripinargentina. "This is my way of showing it from within, as someone who was bBuenosAires_dual_v2orn and raised on its streets." From a renowned 18th-century opera house to a weekly celebration of Argentinian gaucho (country life) culture, Gabriel shows off the can't-miss experiences that make him so passionate about his home city.

GUIDE TO AZORES, PORTUGAL 🇵🇹 “Lush pastures, black-sand beaches, deep lakes and plunging waterfalls are the islands’ calling card,” says Lonely Planet contributor Sandra Henriques. But, maybe most importantly, “the landscapes change from island to island, which is why ‘seen one, seen them all’ doesn’t apply here.” Link in bio for more #BestinTravel picks, our guide to the best places to visit in 2026 🔗

“If you love a good flower field, Iceland in summer should rank at the top of your list,” says Lonely Planet contributor Bailey Freeman (@the_traveling_b). “The island country takes on a blanket of purple blooms that contrast strikingly with the black volcanic soil and soft green moss that are characteristic of its environment.” Get more botanical recommendations from our expert travelers at the link in bio 🔗

In Cartagena — the Colombian coast’s crown jewel — a special magic comes from the confluence of AfroCaribbean, indigenous and Spanish influences. Each is reflected in the music, art and food, including street fruit vendors (known as fruteros) like these ones.

📸: @juantapiasphoto

GUIDE TO BRITISH COLUMBIA, CANADA 🇨🇦 Massive mountains, wild forests and stunning coastlines: here, says Lonely Planet contributor Brendan Sainsbury, “there are innumerable places to witness this outdoor theater, plus plenty of towns and cities to recharge your energy afterwards.” Tap the link in bio for all things #BestInTravel, our top places to visit this year.

"Magical doesn’t come close to describing how fun it was to play in the snow, cozy up in the dark and watch wonder light up the sky all night long," says Lonely Planet's @sahzoo, who took a dream trip to see the northern lights in Fairbanks, Alaska last winter with @borealisbasecamp. But she wasn't alone: she went with her husband, plus tiny humans, ages 11 and 5, who loved every second. There was ice fishing, walking with reindeer and UTVing (and lots of unforgettable grins all around). Link in bio for her full itinerary 🔗

"What strikes me most is the deep sense of community, with people traveling from all corners of the country to spend a week with family, taking part in traditions passed down over generations," says Lonely Planet Correspondent @sheneller, who got the chance to experience Sinhalese and Tamil New Year celebrations, which usually fall on April 13-14 every year, in a rural Sri Lankan village about five hours from her home city of Colombo. "The best part? This is a festival open to everyone," Shenelle says. "So even as a traveler, you get to experience the traditions and culinary delights firsthand."

"Each statue has a different message themed to climate change and how we impact the planet – I loved seeing how each one connects to the bigger picture and message," says Lonely Planet's @acacia_gabriel, who snorkeled through @museomusamx, an underwater museum off the coast of Cancun with over 500 life-size sculptures that encourage coral growth, with @paditv and @pocnadivecenter. "Travel can be such a powerful force for good when we support experiences that promote conservation and/or cultural exchange."

All the pieces are there—figuring out how they go together is the hard part. See if you have what it takes with Pixellence, the newest game from TED (no distractions included). Visit the link in our bio to play now.

AI agents — independent AIs that can think, make decisions, and take actions on their own — are on the rise, and thanks to OpenClaw founder Peter Steinberger, making your own is now more accessible than ever. In this brand new TED Talk from TED2026, he shares the origins of OpenClaw, his strong belief in it’s potential as an open-source software and how agents built by all of us will become even more transformational than chatbots. Visit the link in our bio to learn more.

The American workforce was not built for the AI disruption, says @ginaraimondo, former Governor of Rhode Island and US Secretary of Commerce. But there’s still time to prepare for what’s next. In her #TED2026 Talk, she gives an unflinching review of AI’s impact on work, education and the economy — and offers real solutions to restructure, regroup and adapt for this new era. Visit the link in our bio to watch the full talk.

Collaborating with people whose minds think differently from your own can produce wonders, says Alex Rosenthal, editorial director of @TEDEdAnimation and TED Games. In his TED Talk, he breaks down the mind’s eye spectrum — from aphantasia, the inability to picture anything at all, to hyperphantasia, the ability to visualize in extreme detail. Do you have one of these conditions or do you fall somewhere in between? Visit the link in our bio to watch the full talk.

If you feel frustrated that creating real change  is an uphill battle, you’re not alone. In this moving first talk from #TED2026, Nobel laureate Malala Yousafzai (@malala) shares what she has learned after a decade of advocating for girls’ education, the moment she lost hope and the 3 lessons she learned about working for the future you want. Visit the link in our bio to watch the full talk.

What will the future of learning and careers look like in this new age of AI? Here’s TED Vision Steward and Khan Academy CEO Sal Khan with an exciting announcement about a brand new collaboration between TED, @KhanAcademy and ETS (@etsinsights) designed to help prepare the next generation with the skills they need to thrive in work, life and society amid rapid technological change. Visit the link in TED’s bio to learn more about the Khan TED Institute and how you can get involved.

Last year, we invited all of you to pitch us your biggest, boldest ideas — and from thousands of submissions, we now reach the final 10 — and you’re invited to see them share their talks LIVE this Thursday, April 16 at 11:45AM ET — directly from TED2026. Visit the link our bio to RSVP to be notified as soon as we go live on YouTube.

Can individual actions actually make a difference in the fight against climate change? According to behavioral scientist Jiaying Zhao, not only are they important for pushing towards structural changes, but they can make you feel happier, too. In her TED Talk, she introduces "happy climate hacks" — small, easy shifts like rethinking how you store veggies or carpooling to work — as a way for you take make changes at home that feel sustainable and have a real impact. Visit the link in @TED’s bio to watch her full talk.

You've likely seen handmade signs like these today, and chances are, they made your life just a little easier. In her TED Talk, designer Kate Canales shares how she’s been photographing improvised signage for 20 years — from perplexing bathroom directions to our struggles with doors — and why she sees them as evidence that we still need each other out in the world. “The person who came before us took the time to leave some instructions to make sure that our experience is a little bit better than theirs,” she says. “And that is a sign of humanity that I am always happy to stumble upon.” Visit the link in our bio to see more charming signs.

Why you should blast your abs.⁠
⁠
Host: @manoushz • Manoush Zomorodi/NPR⁠
Producer: @nickolaihammar • Nickolai Hammar/NPR #tedradiohour

There is a sweet spot between planning and procrastination. In this TED Talk, organizational psychologist @AdamGrant explains how original thinkers — like Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and Steve Jobs — are quick to start but slow to finish, leaving themselves open to more possibilities while expanding their creativity. “Originals are not that different from the rest of us,” he says. Visit the link in @TED’s bio to watch the full talk.

Your stress is less about what you’re facing and more about believing you can cope, says psychologist Jenny Taitz (@drjennytaitz). In her TED Talk, she shares 3 resets you can use in stressful situations to stop your thoughts from spiraling, take control of your emotions and reclaim your resilience. Visit the link in our bio to learn more.
